phases.name: Denormalize owner_id onto subtype tables (schema + safe migration)

steps.name: Add owner_id column + index to the four subtype schemas
criteria: thoughts, labels, relationships, chunks each export ownerId ('owner_id', uuid, notNull) referencing users.id onDelete cascade, mirroring projectMeta.ownerId
each table declares a btree index idx_<table>_owner_id on owner_id (idx_thoughts_owner_id, idx_labels_owner_id, idx_relationships_owner_id, idx_chunks_owner_id)
each schema header comment reframes owner_id as a denormalized immutable copy of the project's owner, carried locally so RLS enforces ownership without joining project_meta
owner_id column/index shape matches projectMeta.ownerId and the project_id denormalization exactly (notNull, FK users.id cascade, single-column btree)
EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: static schema metadata, not runtime behavior; backfill-correctness property compensates at step 01-02
steps.scenario_name: all four scoped tables expose immutable denormalized owner_id with idx_<table>_owner_id index

steps.name: Migration: add, backfill, constrain owner_id; stamp on insert
criteria: new migration adds nullable owner_id (uuid) to thoughts, labels, relationships, chunks, then backfills via subtype.project_id = project_meta.id -> project_meta.owner_id before any NOT NULL
per-table statement order is ADD nullable, UPDATE backfill, SET NOT NULL, ADD FK users.id cascade, CREATE INDEX idx_<table>_owner_id, so existing rows never violate NOT NULL
thoughts/labels/relationships create() and pipeline chunkAndEmbed() stamp owner_id (resolved once from the project owner) on their INSERT within the existing transaction; no method UPDATEs owner_id
EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: exact golden DDL; compensating fast-check property (strict=true) asserts every backfilled subtype.owner_id equals its project_meta.owner_id over generated fixtures
steps.scenario_name: Property: backfilled subtype owner_id equals owning project_meta owner_id for every row

phases.name: Non-owner DB role + per-request tenant context wiring

steps.name: Create non-owner app_user login role; connect runtime as it
criteria: new migration issues CREATE ROLE app_user LOGIN, GRANT USAGE on schema + sequences, and GRANT SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on the six app tables; app_user owns nothing so RLS applies without FORCE
schema declares pgRole('app_user').existing() and exports it so drizzle-kit references the role rather than recreating it
DatabaseService connects the runtime Pool via a separate app_user string (DATABASE_URL_APP); drizzle.config.ts and migrations keep the owner DATABASE_URL
a connectivity check confirms app_user runs DML on the app tables and is not a member of the owner role, so it cannot bypass RLS
EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: static role grants and connection wiring; the cross-tenant property at 03-03 is the compensating behavioral proof
steps.scenario_name: app_user role holds only DML grants, owns no tables, and runtime connects as app_user

steps.name: Add asUser/asSystem tenant-context wrappers via AsyncLocalStorage
criteria: DatabaseService.asUser(userId, cb) opens a transaction, runs set_config('app.current_user_id', <userId>, true) (SET LOCAL semantics), then runs cb with the scoped transaction handle
DatabaseService.asSystem(cb) runs cb in a transaction WITHOUT setting app.current_user_id, for background/gateway jobs that legitimately operate outside one tenant context
an AsyncLocalStorage store holds the current userId; the JWT guard populates it from payload.sub for every authenticated request, and service paths run inside asUser automatically
fire-and-forget pipeline/gateway work (chunkAndEmbed, rechunk, event publication) runs under asSystem, never leaking a stale tenant context across the async boundary
fast-check property tests (strict=true): for arbitrary userId, asUser sets app.current_user_id to that uuid and unsets on exit; asSystem never sets it; single-example Jest only as // bypass: fallback
steps.scenario_name: Property: asUser scopes app.current_user_id to the request user and asSystem omits it

phases.name: Define and verify RLS policies for owner isolation and public read

steps.name: Enable drizzle role management; add project_meta RLS policies
criteria: drizzle.config.ts sets entities: { roles: true } (alongside existing schema/out/dialect/dbCredentials) so drizzle-kit manages the app_user role and policies in generated migrations

project_meta calls .enableRLS() and declares, via the third-arg callback array, pgPolicy('project_meta_owner_isolation', { for: 'all', to: appUser, using/withCheck: ownerId = current_setting('app.current_user_id', true)::uuid })
project_meta also declares pgPolicy('project_meta_public_read', { for: 'select', to: appUser, using: isPublic = true }) so public projects are readable regardless of tenant context
no pgTable.withRLS() anywhere (v1.0-beta only, won't compile on 0.45.2); policies come from the third-arg array; the ::uuid cast is present since current_setting returns text
a generated migration enables RLS and creates both project_meta policies; EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: golden policy DDL is static, behavioral proof is the cross-tenant property at 03-03
steps.scenario_name: project_meta enables RLS with owner_isolation (for all) and public_read (for select) policies via the 0.45.2 third-arg API

steps.name: Add owner_id-keyed RLS policies to the four subtype tables
criteria: thoughts, labels, relationships, chunks each call .enableRLS() and declare pgPolicy('<table>_owner_isolation', { for: 'all', to: appUser, using/withCheck: ownerId = current_setting('app.current_user_id', true)::uuid }) via the third-arg array
each policy keys on the table's own local owner_id column added in phase 01 (no join to project_meta), with the mandatory ::uuid cast on current_setting
policies are added via the third-arg array alongside the existing index declarations; no pgTable.withRLS() and no FORCE (app_user owns no tables)
a generated migration under apps/api/drizzle/migrations enables RLS and creates all four <table>_owner_isolation policies
EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: golden policy DDL is static schema metadata; the compensating behavioral proof is the cross-tenant enforcement property at 03-03
steps.scenario_name: thoughts, labels, relationships, and chunks enable RLS with owner_isolation policies keyed on local owner_id

steps.name: Cross-tenant integration test: zero rows for the second user
criteria: integration test seeds two users each owning a project with thoughts, labels, relationships, and chunks, then connects as app_user and runs reads inside asUser(userA)
under asUser(userA), SELECT over project_meta, thoughts, labels, relationships, chunks returns only userA's rows and ZERO of userB's rows (using clause enforced)
under asUser(userA), an INSERT/UPDATE writing a userB-owned row is rejected (withCheck), and a public project owned by userB IS readable via public_read
assertOwnership is retained unchanged in all services during this phase as defense-in-depth, so the test proves RLS isolation independently before any removal in phase 04
this step is integration (verifies policy enforcement against a real Postgres, not an in-memory invariant), single-example by design per the test paradigm; it is NOT property-based
steps.scenario_name: a second user's tenant context returns zero rows and is denied writes for every RLS-protected table

phases.name: Slim redundant app-layer ownership checks, retain project-type FK invariant

steps.name: Remove RLS-redundant ownership checks; retain project-type FK invariant
criteria: remove the ownership role of assertOwnership (caller owns the project) from RLS-covered read/write paths in thoughts, labels, relationships, pipeline services, yielding net code reduction
retain the project-TYPE FK invariant assertOwnership enforced (project_id must reference a project-type entity) wherever a DTO projectId is accepted on create, so a non-project id is still rejected
thought.schema.ts and label.schema.ts header comments note the project-type FK check is the sole remaining app-layer ownership-adjacent check, with owner isolation now enforced by RLS

behavior unchanged: NotFoundException still thrown for missing ids, remove() still cascades, cross-project relationship rejection and endpoint-type validation untouched; the 03-03 cross-tenant test still passes

fast-check property tests (strict=true): for arbitrary DTOs, create() rejects a non-project-type projectId and accepts a valid one; removing the check preserves NotFound/cascade behavior; single-example Jest only as // bypass: fallback
steps.scenario_name: Property: services drop RLS-redundant ownership checks yet still reject non-project-type projectId on create

validation.reviewer: orchestrator-review (automated quality gate + roadmap-only integrity + RLS correctness review)
validation.notes: Quality gate PASS (step-id format, no private-method AC coupling, no identical-pattern batching, schema-valid). files_to_modify omitted per validated house schema (files named in criteria + implementation_scope; matches delivered denormalize-project-id roadmap). Upstream issue UI-1 (pipeline writes vs chunks RLS) resolved: run pipeline under asUser(resolvedOwnerId). See upstream-issues.md.
